items.title: Alles over de wanprestatie van AZ en de kraker Ajax - NEC
items.summary: Wederom geen reden tot vrolijkheid wat betreft de prestaties van Nederland in Europa. Als enige overgebleven club uit ons land ging AZ donderdagavond onderuit, en wel bij FC Noah in de Conference League. Genoeg redenen voor clubwatcher Simon Zwartkruis en Marco Timmer om uitgebreid na te praten met presentator Kalum van Oudheusden. Ook wordt er alvast vooruitgekeken naar Ajax - NEC in de Eredivisie.

items.title: De derde club van Parijs komt eraan
items.summary: Red Star FC mengt zich dit seizoen nadrukkelijk in de strijd om promotie naar de Ligue 1. Wint de derde club van Parijs vrijdag op bezoek bij Annecy, dan is de koppositie een feit. Meer dan een halve eeuw na het laatste avontuur op het hoogste niveau broeit er weer iets in Saint-Ouen. Waarom Red Star afwijkt van Paris Saint-Germain en Paris FC.

items.title: Madrid send UEFA evidence in Vini Jr. racism probe
items.summary: Real Madrid say they have supplied "all available evidence" to UEFA as the governing body investigates allegations of racism made by Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Fabregas 'not proud' of incident with Milan player
items.summary: Como coach Cesc Fabregas has apologised for making contact with an AC Milan player near the touchline in Wednesday's 1-1 league draw at the San Siro.

items.title: 19 sentenced to jail for AFCON final hooliganism
items.summary: A Moroccan court Thursday sentenced 19 soccer fans to up to a year in prison on charges of hooliganism stemming from a chaotic Africa Cup of Nations final between Morocco and Senegal that saw supporters try to storm the field.

items.title: North London derby buildup, Europa League reaction, Premier League team news and more – live
items.summary: ⚽ Latest news, previews and updates before the weekend ⚽ 10 things to look out for | And email Tom Eddie Howe had some words of praise for Pep Guardiola and Manchester City: ““Genuinely, I think we learn something more about ourselves and our game going forward every time we play them. They have been the benchmark for a number of years for many teams. Each painful defeat we suffer at the Etihad we try to grow from it, evolve and improve. “They have been very, very good and are led by an outstanding manager. They continue to be the benchmark in my opinion.” Continue reading...

items.title: Brighton latest: Hurzeler urges positivity amid tough run
Juventus brengt vierde, nostalgisch shirt van 160 euro uit
items.summary: Juventus treedt komend weekend in een gloednieuw tenue aan tegen Como in de Serie A. Met het vierde tenue doet Bianconeri zijn bijnaam eer aan, aangezien het een iconische, wit-zwarte streep bevat.

items.title: Neymar denkt na over pensioen en zet alles op alles om WK te halen
items.summary: Neymar (34) sluit niet uit dat hij aan het einde van 2026 met voetbalpensioen gaat. Het contract van de Braziliaanse superster bij Santos loopt in december af en de mogelijkheid dat hij er dan de brui aan geeft, is aanwezig. Al wil hij nu nog niets toezeggen.

items.title: Zinchenko nam afscheid van Ajax: 'Dat was best wel een gevoelsmomentje'
items.summary: Oleksandr Zinchenko heeft Amsterdam weer achter zich gelaten. De kersverse aanwinst van Ajax revalideert elders van de zware blessure die hij zaterdag opliep tegen Fortuna Sittard. De Oekraïner voelt zich vooral schuldig, vertelde trainer Fred Grim.

Is the new UWCL format easier for English teams?
items.summary: Three English teams have reached the quarter-finals of the Women's Champions League this season - but has it been as easy as it appears?

items.title: How do Brentford keep appointing the right managers?
items.summary: Keith Andrews is impressing at Brentford in his first season as a manager, but how do the Bees keep getting their appointments so right?

items.title: McIlroy masters tricky conditions at Genesis Invitational
items.summary: Rory McIlroy survived a tumultuous opening day in Los Angeles to sit right near the top of the leaderboard at the Genesis Invitational - one shot shy of England's Aaron Rai, who finished two holes shy of a full round.

items.title: Managing Man Utd the 'ultimate role' - Carrick
items.summary: Manchester United caretaker manager Michael Carrick speaks to BBC Sport about trusting academy graduate and 'huge talent' Kobbie Mainoo.

items.title: Schmeichel in spotlight after night of gloom for Celtic
items.summary: BBC Scotland looks at the fallout from Kasper Schmeichel's performance in Celtic' Stuttgart defeat.

items.title: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Tottenham’s Tudor age begins with a north London derby, Guardiola ponders Haaland’s role and Wirtz has a chance to flummox Forest Aston Villa, third in the Premier League, chasing Champions League qualification and the Europa League title, will be expected to beat Leeds on home soil. But Unai Emery’s side have struggled of late in games where the pressure is on and the onus is upon them to be the aggressor. After exiting the FA Cup to Newcastle, Marco Bizot’s moment of madness all but ending their hopes of reaching the fifth round, it is back to league duty. They eked out an ugly win over Brighton, just the kind of result they would be happy with this weekend, but recently they also lost at home to 10-man Brentford and to Everton. Before that they drew at lowly Crystal Palace, though Oliver Glasner’s side have been a bogey team for Villa. This week Bizot apologised for his rush of blood. Which Villa will turn up against Leeds? Ben Fisher Aston Villa v Leeds, Saturday 3pm (all times GMT) Brentford v Brighton, Saturday 3pm Chelsea v Burnley, Saturday 3pm West Ham v Bournemouth, Saturday 5.30pm Continue reading...

items.summary: Noahkai Banks' strong form in Bundesliga might get him a starting spot on Mauricio Pochettino's USMNT squad.

items.title: No respite for World Cup fans affected by Trump travel restrictions
items.summary: Members of the Haitian and Senegalese communities in the U.S. react to President Donald Trump's visa restrictions ahead of the World Cup.
